#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

if [[ $# -lt 1 ]]; then
  echo "usage: $0 <integration-test-path> [device-id]" >&2
  exit 1
fi

target_path="$1"
device_id="${2:-${ANDROID_DEVICE_ID:-emulator-5554}}"
repo_root="${REPO_ROOT:-/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion}"
bridge_url="${LIVE_BRIDGE_BASE_URL:-http://127.0.0.1:3310}"
workspace_path="${LIVE_WORKSPACE_PATH:-$repo_root}"
app_id="${ANDROID_APP_ID:-com.example.vibe_bridge}"

adb -s "$device_id" reverse tcp:3310 tcp:3310
adb -s "$device_id" shell am force-stop "$app_id" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true

declare -a extra_defines
case "$target_path" in
  apps/mobile/integration_test/live_claude_code_title_generation_test.dart|\
  apps/mobile/integration_test/live_claude_code_approval_edit_test.dart)
    extra_defines=(
      "--dart-define=LIVE_CLAUDE_APPROVAL_BRIDGE_BASE_URL=$bridge_url"
      "--dart-define=LIVE_CLAUDE_APPROVAL_WORKSPACE=$workspace_path"
    )
    ;;
  apps/mobile/integration_test/live_codex_thread_creation_test.dart|\
  apps/mobile/integration_test/live_codex_thread_status_order_test.dart)
    extra_defines=(
      "--dart-define=LIVE_CODEX_THREAD_CREATION_BRIDGE_BASE_URL=$bridge_url"
      "--dart-define=LIVE_CODEX_THREAD_CREATION_WORKSPACE=$workspace_path"
    )
    ;;
  *)
    echo "unsupported live target: $target_path" >&2
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

cd "$repo_root/apps/mobile"

flutter drive \
  --driver=test_driver/integration_test.dart \
  --target="${target_path#apps/mobile/}" \
  -d "$device_id" \
  "${extra_defines[@]}"

content.text:
I changed the completion path in [thread_detail_controller.dart](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile/lib/features/threads/application/thread_detail_controller.dart#L528) so Codex no longer snapshot-refreshes the visible timeline on every terminal status. The controller now tracks whether the active turn actually streamed meaningful live activity and whether the stream went unhealthy; on completion it does a detail-only refresh for healthy turns, and keeps the full timeline snapshot refresh only as a fallback for failed/interrupted or suspicious completions. That is the stability/smoothness tradeoff we wanted: no normal finish flash, but recovery is still there when live delivery was incomplete.

I also added focused regressions in [reconnect_retry_scheduling_test.dart](/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile/test/features/threads/reconnect_retry_scheduling_test.dart#L340) to prove both sides:
- healthy live completion does not re-fetch the timeline page
- completion without meaningful live activity still does the authoritative snapshot catch-up

Validation:
- `cd /Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile && flutter test test/features/threads/reconnect_retry_scheduling_test.dart`
- `cd /Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile && flutter analyze lib/features/threads/application/thread_detail_controller.dart test/features/threads/reconnect_retry_scheduling_test.dart`
- `/Users/lubomirmolin/.codex/skills/codex-mobile-live-e2e/scripts/run_live_test.sh apps/mobile/integration_test/live_codex_thread_status_order_test.dart R3CY90LAMTP`

The Samsung run passed. The live thread was `codex:019d48ee-e8b4-7a93-ad68-d648f0d753ef`, and the device logs again showed continuous `message_delta` streaming while the turn was still `running`.
